content: The intrinsic reward signal generated by the ICM curiosity module can act as a dynamic "memory gate" that sorts which novel experiences an autonomous agent stores in short-term memory versus consolidates into long-term memory: only experiences that trigger high intrinsic curiosity (unexpected, learnable new patterns) are prioritized for long-term retention, while low-curiosity predictable interactions are discarded after short-term use. This solves the common problem of long-term memory bloat in autonomous agents by tying memory retention to the agent's current learning goals rather than static storage limits.
